perturbation.original_text: (n) "Listings" shall mean the listing of the names, addresses, pricing and -------- other identifying information of each MBE Center which is a party to a Subscription Agreement (as defined in Section 3(b) [Service]).
perturbation.changed_text: (n) "Listings" shall mean the listing of the names and addresses of each MBE Center which is a party to a Subscription Agreement (as defined in Section 3(b) [Service]).
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'pricing and other identifying information' from the definition of 'Listings' in Section 1(n), it contradicts Section 9(a) which mandates MBE to supply and update 'pricing' information, creating ambiguity on what is to be listed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Listings. MBE shall supply and regularly update the Listings, and the -------- Company shall include the Listings in the Company Site and shall use commercially reasonable best efforts to include such Listings on other third party web sites utilizing the Company Technology.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits "pricing and other identifying information" from the definition of 'Listings'. The contradicted text, Section 9(a), explicitly states that "MBE shall supply and regularly update the Listings". The omission of 'pricing' from the definition directly contradicts the requirement to supply 'pricing' information, making the obligation in Section 9(a) ambiguous and potentially unenforceable as pricing is no longer clearly included as part of 'Listings'.

perturbation.original_text: (u) "Specifications" shall mean the specifications for the Manifest set -------------- forth on Exhibit D, as updated from time to time during the term of this --------- Agreement in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: (u) "Specifications" shall mean a general description of the Manifest. --------------
perturbation.explanation: By simplifying the definition of 'Specifications' in Section 1(u), removing the reference to Exhibit D and updates, it contradicts Section 2(c), Section 4(a) and Section 11(a), since those sections refers to specific functionalities in Exhibit D. This alteration introduces uncertainty about the actual technical requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Inspection. Following the completion of the beta roll-out and testing ---------- of the Service, MBE shall have a period of twenty (20) business days to undertake inspection and testing of the Manifest to determine conformance with applicable Specifications. The Company shall provide any assistance reasonably requested by MBE in assessing such conformance. If MBE finds that any part of the Manifest does not conform with the applicable Specifications, MBE shall, within the inspection period, notify the Company and provide a detailed written description of such nonconformance. Following confirmation by the Company of such nonconformance, the Company will alter the Manifest within a reasonable time to correct such nonconformance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Specifications' by removing the reference to Exhibit D, which contains the detailed technical requirements for the Manifest. The contradicted text, Section 2(c), requires the MBE to inspect the Manifest for conformance with 'applicable Specifications'. Because the Changed Text has simplified the definition of 'Specifications', this creates a contradiction, since the 'Specifications' can no longer be referenced with Exhibit D, introducing uncertainty as to the 'applicable Specifications' and making it impossible to determine conformance. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, since the removal of essential language creates a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Delivery. The Company shall use its commercially reasonable best -------- efforts to complete the design of the Manifest and the Service in a timely and professional manner consistent with commercial software industry standards on or before August 1, 1999 and deliver the URL and the access codes or passwords necessary to use the Service to MBE and each subscribing MBE Center on or before September 1, 1999.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Delivery. The Company shall use its commercially reasonable best -------- efforts to deliver the URL and the access codes or passwords necessary to use the Service to MBE and each subscribing MBE Center on or before September 1, 1999.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'to complete the design of the Manifest and the Service in a timely and professional manner consistent with commercial software industry standards on or before August 1, 1999', it now contradicts section 11 (a), since the service must conform to its specifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Warranty. Company represents and warrants that during the term of this -------- Agreement the Service shall confirm to its specifications and the Specifications in all material respects, provided that the Service is properly used in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Subscription Agreement, and shall not contain a higher number of, or more serious errors, than would be expected by a reasonable commercial user of a service similar to the Service.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a requirement to complete the design of the Manifest and Service by a specific date, and the changed text removes that requirement. This directly contradicts section (a), which warrants that the Service will conform to its specifications, because without the design completion requirement, there's no guarantee that the Service will meet any specific specifications or be completed at all, thus rendering the warranty meaningless or unenforceable. The removal impacts the obligation to meet specifications, creating a strong, in-text contradiction as per the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.
